description_en: Directly states doctrinal content; the hymn functions as a sung creed or doctrinal affirmation
detection_markers: Credal formulae; 'verím/věřím'; doctrinal assertions in indicative mood; articles of faith
example: 'Toto jest tělo mé' — direct Eucharistic confession

description_en: Praises God in terms that carry implicit theological claims; theology embedded in worship language
detection_markers: Praise formulae; 'chválme/chvalme'; 'Sláva/Sláwa'; 'Haleluja'; direct address to God/Christ/Spirit; vocative
example: 'O Beránku Boží' — Lamb of God address implies sacrificial/soteriological theology

description_en: Teaches doctrine to the congregation; didactic intent; may use question-answer or expository structure
detection_markers: Imperative + doctrinal content; 'uč se/naučte se'; question-answer structure; numbered articles; explanatory 'neb/nebo/neboť'
example: Hymns with embedded catechetical instruction on sacraments or commandments

description_en: Expresses contrition, confession, plea for forgiveness, or absolution; soteriological self-examination
example: Communion preparation hymns; Lenten penitential songs

description_en: Argues against opposing theological positions; anti-papal, anti-'Sacramentarian', anti-Anabaptist, or inter-confessional critique
detection_markers: 'Proti/contra'; negation of opposing positions; 'falešní/falošní'; 'bludaři'; naming of opponents; rhetorical questions attacking other views
example: Anti-papal hymns; defences of specific Eucharistic positions against 'Sacramentarians'

description_en: Retells biblical or salvation-historical events; the theology is carried by the story rather than by direct assertion
detection_markers: Past tense narration; biblical characters named; sequential events; 'stalo se/když'; Christmas/Easter/Pentecost narratives
example: Christmas hymns retelling the nativity; Easter hymns narrating resurrection

description_en: Personal piety without strong doctrinal specificity; spiritual aspiration, trust, comfort
detection_markers: First person singular; 'duše má/moja'; 'útěcha/útecha'; 'naděje/nádej'; general spiritual aspiration without confessionally distinctive claims
example: General spiritual songs expressing trust in God without confessionally specific content

description_en: Expresses hope of eternal life, anticipation of judgment, longing for the coming kingdom
example: Advent hymns; funeral hymns; hymns anticipating the Last Judgment

description_en: Tied to a specific moment in the liturgical action (Introit, Gradual, Offertory, Communion, etc.); function defined by position in ordo
detection_markers: Rubrical placement; 'při/pri' + liturgical action; 'k přijímání/k prijímaniu'; fixed position in liturgical order
example: Communion hymns sung during distribution; Introit hymns

description_en: Petitions God on behalf of the community; carries ecclesiological and soteriological assumptions in its prayer logic
example: Litanies; prayers for the church; prayers for rulers
